context: Evaluates correct use of the three attachment Tauri commands: attachment_save, attachment_get, and attachment_delete. Tests that binary data is passed correctly to attachment_save, that the returned metadata includes hash_sha256, and that attachment_get returns the original bytes.

checklist.name: attachment_save invocation
checklist.description: The solution calls `attachment_save` with four arguments: `note_id` (UUID string), `filename` (string), `mime` (string), and `data` (byte array / Uint8Array / number[]).

checklist.name: Attachment metadata fields
checklist.description: The solution reads the returned `Attachment` object and accesses `id`, `filename`, `mime`, `size_bytes`, and `hash_sha256` fields.

checklist.name: attachment_get invocation
checklist.description: The solution calls `attachment_get` with the attachment UUID and receives the decrypted binary data (byte array matching the original input).

checklist.name: attachment_delete invocation
checklist.description: The solution calls `attachment_delete` with the attachment UUID to remove the attachment.
